verdict.answer_first: Tie: both state the core actionable cause immediately, A: "EADDRINUSE means the port is already bound", B: "EADDRINUSE means a socket is already bound".
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly more direct with one primary diagnostic: "run ... lsof -nP -iTCP:3000 -sTCP:LISTEN"; B surveys more cases before ending with triage.
verdict.fabrication: Both invent machine-specific context; A says "you run a PM2/launchd bridge (`mac-bridge-server`)" and B says "on *your* machine specifically — you run Node under a PM2/launchd bridge".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it gives the lsof command plus what to do if the PID respawns or if lsof shows nothing.
